prompt.content:
Use file “ONC_PATIENT 7” to access the entire medical history / record for the patient. Please use the patient tab as the source of truth for all demographic information. Disregard patient identifiers (Name, Age, DOB, MRN) found within the text of the clinicalnotes tab as they have been shifted for de-identification purposes

------

For the purpose of this scenario, please assume that the date of birth of the patient is January 1, 1930.

A patient previously treated for lung cancer has had recent surgery following changes on a surveillance CT scan. She is seen in the multidisciplinary clinic several weeks later with her partner, on the 5th of January, 2026.

According to the RESECT-90 criteria, please calculate the patient's 90-day mortality risk as a percentage to the nearest 10th decimal place, and explain your rationale.

extra.task_name: Labs_Image_AI Scribe_ONC Patient 7 - RESECT-90 - Chris B Writer

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
For the RESECT-90 score, the following clinical parameters exist for the calculation:

The patient is 96 years old.
The patient is female.
The patient has an ECOG performance score of 2.
The patient has a predicted diffusion capacity of the lung for carbon monoxide percentage of 45%.
The patient has a body mass index of 22.6 kg/m^2 (calculated from weight 50.8 kg, height 1.499 m)
The patient has a creatinine of 141 μmol/L.
The patient has anemia.
The patient has an arrhythmia (atrial fibrillation).
The patient has had a right-sided resection.
The patient has had 3 bronchopulmonary segments resected.
The patient had thoracoscopic surgery, not an open thoracotomy.
The patient has a malignancy.

Based on this information, the patient has a 90-day mortality of 25.6%.
